page: Microsoft Azure E48pds_v6 is an ARM-based virtual machine belonging to the Epdsv6 server family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 48 dedicated physical cores powered by the Microsoft Neoverse-N2 processor architecture running at 3.4 GHz, with no hyperthreading. The hardware configuration includes 384.0 GB of RAM, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. Local storage is bundled with a capacity of 2835 GB, and the instance is Premium Storage capable.

Benchmark data reveals top-tier memory performance, with uncached large-block read bandwidth reaching 463.82 GB/sec and write bandwidth at 199.65 GB/sec. Multi-core processing is strong, as evidenced by a Geekbench multi-core score of 21816.0 and a stress-ng multi-core CPU performance of 99354.67. Database operations are highly efficient, with Passmark database operations scoring in the top 10% at 37978.53 and Redis SET operations processing 89.5 million operations per second.

While multi-core and memory-bound tasks perform exceptionally well, single-core performance is moderate, with a Geekbench single-core score of 1692.0. Large-scale LLM prompt processing represents a notable bottleneck, with the 70B model prompt processing benchmark falling into the bottom 25% at 6.9 tokens/sec. This server is optimized for memory-intensive workloads, database management, static web serving, and multi-threaded software compilation rather than heavy single-threaded execution or large-scale LLM inference.
description: Microsoft Azure E48pds_v6 is an ARM-based virtual machine from the Epdsv6 family, utilizing 48 dedicated physical cores of the Microsoft Neoverse-N2 processor running at 3.4 GHz. It features 384.0 GB of RAM, providing an 8.0 GB per core memory-to-core ratio, alongside 2835 GB of local storage. Operating on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this instance delivers top-tier memory bandwidth, with uncached large-block reads at 463.82 GB/sec and writes at 199.65 GB/sec. Multi-core benchmarks are strong, highlighted by a Geekbench multi-core score of 21816.0 and Passmark database operations in the top 10%. However, single-core performance is average, and large-scale LLM prompt processing is weak. This hardware profile is suited for memory-intensive databases, multi-threaded software compilation, and high-throughput web serving.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E48pds_v6 is an ARM-based virtual machine featuring 48 dedicated Neoverse-N2 cores, 384 GB of RAM, and 2835 GB of local storage, optimized for memory-intensive databases and multi-core workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E48pds_v6 is an ARM-based virtual machine with 48 dedicated cores, 384 GB RAM, and 2835 GB local storage for database workloads.
tagline: An ARM-powered virtual machine offering high memory bandwidth, dedicated physical cores, and substantial local storage for data-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: 48 dedicated ARM Neoverse-N2 physical cores running at 3.4 GHz
384.0 GB of system memory with top-tier read and write bandwidth
2835 GB of bundled local storage with Premium Storage capability
Strong multi-core performance and high-throughput database operations
Average single-core execution and limited large-scale LLM inference capability
